validation: The page loads the supplied Pixels Medium TTF and visibly reports when it falls back to the embedded outline atlas. Preserving its grid corners, collapsing repeated bridge points, and retaining Mesh-to-Curve wire provenance keeps the authored, subdivide=1, and subdivide=0 sweeps topology-exact. A 12,361-point Blender probe confirms Blur Attribute keeps the center at weight 1 and uses the current element's Weight for every adjacent contribution; the browser now matches that one-pass vector result bit-for-bit. After all 334 authored passes, the three sweep bounds differ from Blender by only 0.0095, 0.0094, and 0.0101 units respectively. The separate 3D Chrome regression was traced to extraction-only font sampling metadata crossing Realize Instances, not Blur Attribute; filtering that private marker preserves exact geometry in all six 3D Chrome cases.
